message.abstract:
<jats:p>
                  Two methods of calculating the reproduction index from daily new infection data are considered, one by using the generation time
                  <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>G</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  as a shift (
                  <jats:monospace>R</jats:monospace>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>G</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  ), and an incidence-based method directly derived from the differential equation system of an SIR epidemic dynamics model (
                  <jats:monospace>R</jats:monospace>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>I</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  ). While the former is shown to have few in common with the true reproduction index, we find that the latter provides a sensitive detection device for intervention effects and other events affecting the epidemic, making it well-suited for diagnostic purposes in policy making. Furthermore, we introduce a similar quantity,
                  <jats:inline-formula>
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:inline-graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22273599v1_inline1.gif"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:inline-formula>
                  , which can be calculated directly from
                  <jats:monospace>R</jats:monospace>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>G</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  . It shows largely the same behaviour as
                  <jats:monospace>R</jats:monospace>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>I</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  , with less fine structure. However, it is accurate in particular in the vicinity of
                  <jats:monospace>R</jats:monospace>
                  = 1, where accuracy is important for the corrrect prediction of epidemic dynamics. We introduce an entirely new, self-consistent method to derive, from both quantities, an improved
                  <jats:inline-formula>
                    <jats:alternatives>
                      <jats:inline-graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="22273599v1_inline2.gif"/>
                    </jats:alternatives>
                  </jats:inline-formula>
                  which is both accurate and contains the details of the epidemic spreading dynamics. Hence we obtain
                  <jats:monospace>R</jats:monospace>
                  accurately from data on daily new infections (incidence) alone. Moreover, by using
                  <jats:monospace>R</jats:monospace>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>I</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  instead of
                  <jats:monospace>R</jats:monospace>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>G</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  in plots of
                  <jats:monospace>R</jats:monospace>
                  versus incidence, orbital trajectories of epidemic waves become visible in a particularly insightful way, demonstrating that the widespread use of only incidence as a diagniostic tool is clearly inappropriate.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>PACS numbers:</jats:p>
title: The incidence-based dynamic reproduction index: accurate determination, diagnostic sensitivity, and predictive power

reference.journal-title: Asia Pacific Journal of Public Administration
Rev. sci. tech. Off. int. Epiz

reference.unstructured: J. Dehning , J. Zierenberg , F. P. Spitzner , M. Wibral , J. P. Neto , M. Wilczek , and V. Priesemann (2020), URL https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.09.16.20187484;.
Robert Koch Institut, Aktueller Lage-/Situationsbericht des RKI zu COVID-19 (2020), URL https://www.rki.de/DE/Content/InfAZ/

J. Dehning , F. P. Spitzner , M. C. Linden , S. B. Mohr , J. P. Neto , J. Zierenberg , M. Wibral , M. Wilczek , and V. Priesemann , Science 369 (2020), ISSN 0036-8075, URL https://science.sciencemag.org/content/early/2020/05/14/science.abb9789.
RKI, Tech. Rep., Robert Koch Institut, Berlin (2020).
M. an der Heiden and O. Hamouda , Epidemiol. Bull. pp. 10–16 (2020), ISSN 2569-5266.
Robert Koch Institut, Nowcasting und R-Schätzung: Schätzung URL https://www.rki.de/DE/Content/InfAZ/N/Neuartiges{\_}Coronavirus/Projekte{\_}RKI/Nowcasting.html.
